$864.215M Dedicated Tax Fund Green Bonds Series 2024B Issuance

payload.event_name: MTA Dedicated Tax Fund Green Bonds, Series 2024B
payload.description: MTA issued $864.215 million of Dedicated Tax Fund Green Bonds, Series 2024B. Proceeds used to retire outstanding TBTA PMT BANs Series 2022B and to lock in net present value saving of $24.381 million or 12.74% of refunded par by refunding certain outstanding Transportation Revenue Bonds.

evidence_refs.source_quote: On October 9, 2024, MTA issued $864.215 million of Dedicated Tax Fund Green Bonds, Series 2024B

tool_args.label: Upcoming $485M Transportation Revenue Refunding Green Bonds Series 2024B

payload.event_name: MTA Transportation Revenue Refunding Green Bonds, Series 2024B
payload.description: MTA expects to issue approximately $485.00 million of Transportation Revenue Refunding Fund Green Bonds, Series 2024B. Proceeds will be used to refund certain outstanding Transportation Revenue Bonds for savings.

evidence_refs.source_quote: On October 30, 2024, MTA expects to issue approximately $485.00 million of Transportation Revenue Refunding Fund Green Bonds, Series 2024B

tool_args.label: YTD Sep 2024 Operating Revenue Drivers

payload.claim_text: Operating revenue for YTD September was $79 million (1%) unfavorable, comprised of unfavorable passenger revenues of $86 million (2%) and unfavorable toll revenue of $6 million (less than 1%), partially offset by favorable other operating revenues of $14 million (2%).

evidence_refs.source_quote: Operating revenue for YTD September was $79 million (1%) unfavorable, comprised of unfavorable passenger revenues of $86 million (2%) and unfavorable toll revenue of $6 million (less than 1%), partially offset by favorable other operating revenues of $14 million (2%).

tool_args.label: YTD Sep 2024 Operating Subsidies Summary

payload.claim_text: Operating Subsidies, which also reflects Investment Income, was favorable for YTD September by $55 million (0.8%). Excluding Investment Income, subsidies directed to the operating budget were favorable by $36 million (0.6%). Driven by favorable results for Urban Tax, Payroll Mobility Tax (PMT), and Mortgage Recording Tax partially offset by unfavorable results for Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE), CDOT, and the For-Hire Vehicle (FHV) Surcharge.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Operating Subsidies, which also reflects Investment Income, was favorable for YTD September by $55 million (0.8%).

tool_args.label: YTD Sep 2024 Expense Favorable Variance Detail

payload.claim_text: Labor expenses were $66 million (1%) favorable, non-labor expenses were $151 million (5%) favorable, and other expense adjustments were $31 million favorable. Within labor expenses, payroll was $139 million (3%) favorable mainly due to vacancies at NYCT, health and welfare $39 million (3%), and OPEB current payments of $31 million (5%), partially offset by unfavorable overtime spending of $194 million (31%). Within non-labor expenses, professional service contracts were $126 million (22%) favorable.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Labor expenses were $66 million (1%) favorable, non-labor expenses were $151 million (5%) favorable, and other expense adjustments were $31 million favorable.

tool_args.label: MTA-Wide Vacancy Composition September 2024

payload.claim_text: On an MTA-Wide basis, there were 2,844 vacancies in September, representing a vacancy rate of (4%), which was primarily comprised of vacancies in operations and maintenance (82% of total vacancies).

evidence_refs.source_quote: On an MTA-Wide basis, there were 2,844 vacancies in September, representing a vacancy rate of (4%), which was primarily comprised of vacancies in operations and maintenance (82% of total vacancies).

tool_args.label: Passenger Revenue Variance Drivers

payload.claim_text: The unfavorable variances primarily reflected unfavorable paid ridership for NYCT (both Subway and Bus), MTA Bus (partially offset by higher average yield), and Staten Island Railway. The LIRR and MNR were both favorable due to higher than budgeted ridership.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The unfavorable variances primarily reflected unfavorable paid ridership for NYCT (both Subway and Bus), MTA Bus (partially offset by higher average yield), and Staten Island Railway. The LIRR and MNR were both favorable due to higher than budgeted ridership.
